rules.section_title: Interactive Notebook: Section 4.1 — 2D Convolution Kernel Demo
rules.description: Adjust the kernel size and values, then step through positions to see how each output value is computed from the element-wise product.

rules.section_title: Interactive Notebook: Section 6.2 — Output Size Formula
rules.description: Vary input size N, filter size F, stride S, and padding P to see the output dimensions change live. Switch to non-square mode for separate height and width controls.

rules.section_title: Interactive Notebook: Section 6.3 — Impact of Stride
rules.description: Change the stride to see which positions the filter visits and how skipping positions reduces the output size.
Adjust zero-padding P to see the padded input and resulting output size. Observe the special case where padding achieves "same" convolution.
Choose between max, average, and L2 pooling. Step through the pooling window to see how each operation downsamples the feature map.

rules.section_title: Interactive Notebook: Section 5 — Vanishing Gradient Problem
rules.description: See how gradient magnitudes shrink through deep networks for different activation functions. Toggle activations and adjust depth to observe vanishing vs stable gradients.
Click any hidden neuron to toggle it on/off and simulate dropout. Track the effective dropout rate and see how the active sub-network changes.
Train a small neural network on a toy dataset and compare how different activation functions affect convergence speed and final accuracy.
Adjust LRN parameters k, α, β, and window n to see how channel activations are normalized. Observe how large activations are suppressed more than small ones.

rules.section_title: Interactive Notebook: Section 8.3.0.1 — Batch Normalizing Transform
rules.description: Adjust batch size, γ (scale), β (shift), and ε to see the three-step BN transform: compute statistics, normalize, then scale and shift.
Compare gradient flow in plain vs residual networks. Vary depth and weight scale to see how skip connections prevent vanishing gradients.
Gradient descent locks onto the saddle point of f(x,y)=x²−y² regardless of learning rate. Toggle modes to see how noise or momentum can escape.
Compare Batch GD, Momentum, AdaGrad, and RMSProp on ill-conditioned loss landscapes. Toggle each optimizer and tune parameters independently.
Compare Batch GD, Momentum, AdaGrad, and RMSProp on ill-conditioned loss landscapes. Toggle each optimizer and tune parameters independently.

rules.section_title: Interactive Notebook: Section 6 — Condition Numbers & Curvature
rules.description: Adjust the Hessian eigenvalues λ₁ and λ₂ to change the condition number κ=λ_max/λ_min. See how anisotropic curvature slows GD convergence.
Adam combines momentum and RMSProp with bias correction. Adjust α, β₁, β₂ and compare against GD, Momentum, and RMSProp.

rules.section_title: Interactive Notebook: Section 4 — CE vs Weighted CE vs Focal Loss
rules.description: Adjust class weights (WCE) and focus parameter γ (Focal Loss) to see how each loss re-weights hard vs easy examples for imbalanced datasets.

rules.section_title: Interactive Notebook: Sections 10–11 — Weight Init & Activation/Gradient Histograms
rules.description: Select initialization scheme (Zeros, Xavier, Kaiming) and activation function to see activation std and gradient norms per layer. Compare healthy vs broken configurations.
rules.iframe_title: Interactive Sections 10-11 Weight Init and Activation Histograms

rules.description: Explore principal components: adjust data angle, variance ratio, and number of points. See how PC arrows align with maximum variance directions and how a linear AE learns the same subspace.

rules.section_title: Interactive Notebook: Sections 2.3–3 — Linear vs Nonlinear Autoencoder
rules.description: Compare linear AE (equivalent to PCA) with nonlinear AEs (Sigmoid, Tanh, ReLU). Adjust latent dimension k and see reconstruction quality and latent space cluster separation.
rules.iframe_title: Interactive Sections 2.3-3 Linear vs Nonlinear AE

rules.description: See all three unpooling strategies side by side: max-unpooling (uses stored indices), bed-of-nails (top-left placement), and nearest-neighbor (fill entire block). Randomize the input to explore different cases.
Step through a transposed convolution one input element at a time. See how each input scales the kernel and accumulates in the output. Formula: H_out = (H_in−1)×s − 2p + k.

rules.section_title: Interactive Notebook: Section 5.4 — Convolutional Autoencoder on MNIST
rules.description: Compare FC-AE and Conv-AE reconstruction quality across latent dimensions. Conv-AEs exploit spatial structure for better reconstructions at smaller latent sizes.

rules.section_title: Interactive Notebook: Section 2.4 — VAE Training (ELBO & Reparameterization)
rules.description: Adjust μ and σ to see how the posterior q(z|x) compares to the prior N(0,1). The KL divergence measures their distance. See the reparameterization trick z=μ+σε and the full ELBO as a function of μ.

rules.section_title: Interactive Notebook: Section 2.4 — VAE Latent Space Explorer
rules.description: Click anywhere in the 2D latent space to decode z directly into a digit — no encoder needed. Explore how different regions generate different digits and interpolate smoothly between them.

rules.section_title: Interactive Notebook: Section 3.3 — Vanishing Gradient in RNNs
rules.description: Vary the number of timesteps T and weight magnitude to see how gradient magnitude decays for sigmoid, tanh, and ReLU activations in BPTT.
